title: Check if a Binary Tree is Height-Balanced
description:
Determine if A binary tree is height-balanced if for every node the heights of the left and right subtrees differ by no more than one. Determine whether a given tree is balanced. Return True if balanced, otherwise False.

    

Constraints:
    - 0 ≤ nodes ≤ 10^5
explanation:
Intuition
Compute subtree heights bottom-up. If any subtree is unbalanced, propagate a sentinel value upward to avoid recomputing heights. This turns balance checks into a single postorder traversal.

Approach
1. Initial Setup: Write a helper returning height or -1 if unbalanced.
2. Main Algorithm: At each node, get left and right heights; if diff > 1 return -1.
3. Return Result: Top-level returns True iff helper returns nonnegative.

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: O(n)
- Space Complexity: O(h) recursion

Key Insights
- Sentinel shortcut avoids extra passes
- Height depends on children's heights

Common Pitfalls
- Using absolute diff on None incorrectly
- Confusing nodes and edges when computing height
hints: Return -1 when a subtree is unbalanced
Use postorder to get heights first
